core_case.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 20?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 20?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: Exact ET-to-Binance minute mapping is clear enough but single-minute settlement mechanics remain operationally sensitive.
core_case.freshness_driver: BTC can reprice sharply over the weekend and the contract resolves on a Monday noon ET minute close.
decision_blockers: No decisive independently verified volatility/base-rate model for the chance of an 8%+ drawdown by settlement.
Single-minute Binance settlement leaves residual venue and microstructure fragility.
Any fresh weekend or Monday-morning macro/crypto shock could materially change the estimate.

structured_handoff_primary.contract_ambiguity_reason: Exact ET-to-Binance minute mapping is clear enough but single-minute settlement mechanics remain operationally sensitive.
independently_verified_points: Binance BTCUSDT spot was about 74230 during the run, well above 68000.
Recent Binance daily context showed BTC mostly above 68k and recent lows above the strike.
The governing source is Binance BTC/USDT final close of the 12:00 ET one-minute candle on April 20.
The main residual risk is short-horizon drawdown plus single-minute single-venue settlement fragility, not current price misread.
structured_handoff_primary.verification_gap_summary: No strong independent quantification of six-day downside-tail frequency from a 74k-to-68k starting point was added beyond lane-level reasoning.
structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: An ordinary crypto drawdown or venue-specific bad print into the exact settlement minute can still flip NO despite BTC being comfortably above strike today.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mostly about how much probability to haircut for minute-specific settlement and crypto downside-tail risk.
structured_handoff_primary.resolution_mechanics_summary: Resolve from Binance BTC/USDT final close of the April 20 12:00 ET one-minute candle; price must be strictly above 68000.
structured_handoff_primary.freshness_driver: BTC can reprice sharply over the weekend and the contract resolves on a Monday noon ET minute close.
decision_blockers: No decisive independently verified volatility/base-rate model for the chance of an 8%+ drawdown by settlement.
Single-minute Binance settlement leaves residual venue and microstructure fragility.
Any fresh weekend or Monday-morning macro/crypto shock could materially change the estimate.

verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker may perform bounded independent source discovery/search during targeted escalation, but only within runtime-enforced tool/query/fetch budgets.

prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

BTC finishing above 68,000 on the April 20 Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close remains the clear base case, but the swarm’s slight-below-market stance still looks right after verification: current spot is comfortably above the strike, yet a six-day crypto contract tied to one exact exchange-minute close does not justify near-certainty as strongly as a 93.5% market implies.

## Why this may matter now

Market implies 93.5% YES; my synthesized range is 87% to 90% YES. That is still a strong YES lean, but the edge versus market is only modest and comes from the view that the market is slightly too confident about a six-day crypto contract settling on one exact Binance minute close. Actionability is marginal rather than screaming because the market is directionally right and the residual edge is mostly calibration.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

This is not materially different from the swarm-implied center; it is essentially a slight widening around the swarm’s 0.88 to 0.89 core. The synthesis-stage verification confirmed the main facts the swarm relied on and did not uncover a reason either to move sharply toward the 0.935 market or farther away from it. …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Market is directionally right that >68k is likely, but 93.5-94% looks somewhat overconfident given six-day BTC volatility and the exact Binance noon-ET minute-close condition.
main_logical_chain: Market implies about 93.5-94% Yes because current Binance spot is comfortably above 68k.
Direct Binance data supports a strong Yes lean because recent spot and range have stayed above the strike.
But the contract is narrow and short-horizon crypto downside tails are not negligible.
So the correct update is high-probability Yes, but modestly below market at 88%.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT traded around 74.2k during the run, about 9.2% above the 68k strike.
Recent Binance daily lows in the sampled week stayed above roughly 70.5k.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still move 8-10% in six days.
Resolution depends on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, adding timing and venue-specific risk.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact short-horizon downside-tail frequency was not quantified in a formal base-rate study.
Noon-ET candle operational mapping appears clear from rules but still matters mechanically.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A move of Binance BTC/USDT back near 70k, a new macro/crypto shock, or evidence of Binance-specific settlement noise would lower the estimate; continued stable trading above 72k would raise it.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on April 20, 2026 at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close, so date/time precision is material.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary contract source and settlement venue data are strong, but evidence independence is only medium and no authoritative future-settling source exists yet.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: YES is likelier because BTC is already well above 68k on Binance, but a six-day crypto hold-above threshold is not quite as safe as the 93.5% market price implies.
main_logical_chain: The governing venue is Binance BTC/USDT and current Binance spot is materially above 68k.
A base-rate prior for a threshold already 8% in-the-money over a six-day horizon favors YES.
Crypto short-horizon volatility and one-minute settlement mechanics still leave more tail risk than a 93.5% market price suggests.
Therefore YES remains likelier, but with a modest discount versus market confidence.
strongest_supports: Binance ticker showed BTCUSDT around 74.23k on 2026-04-14, leaving about a 6.23k cushion above strike.
Recent Binance daily candles show BTC spending most of the recent month above 68k, with the latest cluster mostly above 70k.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still fall more than 8% over six days, especially across a weekend.
The contract settles on one exact noon ET one-minute candle, which increases path and microstructure risk.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether weekend volatility or any macro shock emerges before settlement.
How much practical noise remains in Binance's timezone-sensitive noon ET candle handling.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would cut materially if BTC loses the low-70k region before the weekend or if Binance-specific settlement risk rises; I would move closer to market if BTC still holds above roughly 72k into April 19-20 with no operational concerns.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the contract resolves at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-20, so both timezone mechanics and short-horizon volatility matter.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good enough for a medium-difficulty case: primary rules plus primary Binance data, cross-checked with CoinGecko. Independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is likely to remain above 68000 by the April 20 noon ET Binance minute close, but the market is slightly overconfident because settlement depends on one future exchange-specific minute close rather than broad BTC direction.
main_logical_chain: The rules make Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET 1-minute final close the governing source of truth.
Current Binance spot and recent daily context place BTC materially above 68000, so Yes is the base case.
Because the contract is minute-specific and exchange-specific with six days remaining, confidence should be high but not as high as the market's 93.5%.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was 74233.62 on 2026-04-14, about 9.17% above the threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes sampled for April 10-14 were above 68000.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on a single future one-minute Binance close six days away, leaving meaningful residual path risk.
BTC can plausibly move more than 9% over that horizon in a volatile regime.
unresolved_ambiguities: No future settlement-minute data exists yet, so the final outcome still depends on intervening volatility.
Cross-source independence is only moderate because Binance provides both governing spot family and recent kline context.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if BTC trades back toward 70000 or below before resolution or if Binance-specific irregularity appears; I would move closer to market if BTC stays comfortably above 72000-74000 into April 19-20.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Settlement is specifically the Binance BTCUSDT 1-minute candle close at 2026-04-20 12:00 ET, which was explicitly verified as 16:00 UTC.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is strong for rules and current price context; source-of-truth ambiguity is low, but evidence independence is only moderate.
